questions.scenario: Customers get TLS errors before any request reaches the Cloud Run container. Which map area should the team inspect first?

options.text: Traffic: DNS, certificate, routing, and HTTPS entry.

questions.explanation: TLS errors before the container point to the public entry path. Start with traffic, then continue through the map if needed.

questions.scenario: The app starts, but logs show permission denied when reading `orders-db-url` from Secret Manager. Which map area is first?

options.text: Access: runtime service account, Secret Manager resource, and IAM binding.

questions.explanation: A secret permission error is an access problem. The runtime service account and secret IAM policy are the first evidence to read.

questions.scenario: A release serves the wrong version to some users. Which evidence belongs in the first deployment review?

options.text: Cloud Run revision and traffic split.
Runtime configuration and logs for the revision.

questions.explanation: Release behavior crosses image, revision, config, traffic, and logs. Billing may matter later, but it does not identify which version is serving.
